research.title: ASCO cancer-cachexia guideline (2020): Applicability must be confirmed
research.cite: Roeland et al., J Clin Oncol · DOI 10.1200/JCO.20.00611 · ASCO: low evidence, moderate-strength recommendation (NUT-03)
research.note: This guidance concerns adults with advanced cancer and appetite and/or body-weight loss. Confirm stage ({{PATIENT_SLOT_STAGE}}) and appetite change ({{PATIENT_SLOT_APPETITE_CHANGE}}) before applying it to this synthetic case. It permits referral; it does not mandate one.

supporting.title: Individualized nutrition during colorectal-cancer chemotherapy: Small RCT (2025)
supporting.cite: Wang et al., Nutrition and Cancer · DOI 10.1080/01635581.2025.2449719 · small postoperative colorectal RCT; indirect here (NUT-05)
supporting.note: This 88-person study reports chemotherapy-tolerance and quality-of-life outcomes in an adjacent colorectal population, not this tumor type. It does not establish a survival effect or patient-specific benefit.

research.honesty: NUT-09 is an UNVERIFIED gap statement limited to colon-cancer/FOLFOX patients. It cannot establish the evidence gap for this pending vaginal-cancer case, so this template makes no exact-population RCT claim.

quickScripted: TEMPLATE-ONLY — UNDERDETERMINED: No case-specific quick take is bound to this file. This template makes no survival claim and does not choose referral timing.

seats.name: Registered dietitian
seats.rationale: TEMPLATE-ONLY refusal: ASPEN 2024 identifies MST (at least 2) and PG-SGA-SF (at least 7 or 8) among valid outpatient-oncology cut points (NUT-02), but this file has no bound case score or dietitian stance. Weight loss above 5% is a cachexia diagnostic criterion (NUT-04), not a referral mandate.
seats.requests: Bind the verified weight trajectory, intake estimate, screening score, and patient preference; then rewrite this stance.
